channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Consistent fly tipping in car park, 7th June
item.description:
There is an ongoing fly-tipping issue behind our building, where people regularly dump household waste, old mattresses, furniture, and other bulky items. Despite previous clearances, rubbish continues to accumulate, creating an unsightly environment and potential health and safety concerns. The problem is persistent and recurring, with waste often building up again shortly after it has been removed. We would appreciate further action to help prevent continued illegal dumping in this area.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 167, Rectory Lane, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ballon Cannisters, 7th June
item.description:
Balloon canisters dumped in the flower beds

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Fenham Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Opposite the library outside Queen’s Park flowers, 7th June
item.description:
Hole in the pavement , busy pavement

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salusbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rats, 7th June
item.description:
The alleyway behind Broom Avenue and Dawson has a rat infestation problem. There are so many you now see them constantly running around in daylight. There are also dead rats in the alleyway as well. Please pass onto the relevant person, it's a health concern and needs to be controlled.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 71, Broom Avenue, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Recycling bin overflowing near river, 7th June
item.description:
A recycling bin has been set alight and its contents strewn around the riverbank and in the river in Bexley Woods.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Charter Drive, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly Tipping, 7th June
item.description:
Rubbish dumped on side of pavement see photo.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lesney Park Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress dumped outside where I live, 7th June
item.description:
2 mattresses have been dumped outside where I live, since Friday evening.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): High Street, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Harvington Park Car Park A Complete Mess Again, 7th June
item.description:
This is daily now - people are using the recycling bins as a waste tip and the Council is allowing it. As we live opposite it can our Council Tax be reduced given the Council are allowing an illegal waste tip to operate next to us?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): South Eden Park Road, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins vandalised, 7th June
item.description:
In two locations Two bins have been pulled out of the ground and rolled away from the original areas.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Camdale Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grove Vale- Dumped rubbish, 7th June
item.description:
Outside 19 Grove Vale is dumped rubbish. Needs to be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 19, Melbourne Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lime bike blocking pavement, 7th June
item.description:
The bike is parked right across the sloped pavement, you couldn't do it better if you intended to block the pavement. No way a pushchair/wheelchair/blind person could get round it safely. There is a bike parking bay just 30 metres over the road. The bike(s) are left by people using the business in the arches.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gordon Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 102 Grove Vale-abandoned trolley, 7th June
item.description:
A trolley is blocking the pavement and could end up in the road at a very busy junction. Needs to be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 102, Grove Vale, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Second request, 7th June
item.description:
Wedding needed on the stretch of Glengall Road ! By the curbs and on the pavements! Neglected for a sometime ☹️ Previous report: FixMyStreet ref: 9533471

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glengall Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light and path light out, 7th June

East street E23 1 AND 23 1A

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): East Street, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole getting worse, 7th June
item.description:
This pothole is getting worse. It already presents the risk of damaging wheels and tyres. It will compromise the safety of cyclists and motorcyclists if not repaired. Other speed humps in this road are also breaking up and those outside numbers 33 and 9 also need inspection.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 34, Marlborough Park Avenue, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicle, 7th June
item.description:
This vehicle may have been abandoned. It's MOT expired on 02 Mar 2026. Both wing mirrors are smashed in too.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oakleigh Crescent, Brunswick Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole getting worse, 7th June
item.description:
This pothole is getting bigger. It already presents the risk of damaging wheels and tyres and if it gets any worse could throw a cyclist or motorcyclist onto the road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 44, Marlborough Park Avenue, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 7th June
item.description:
Junction long lane and Bostall park Avenue
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bostall Park Avenue, West Heath, Greater London
